zh.Kinnersley Castle, Hereford, Herefordshi: 英國英格蘭賀福爾金納斯利
zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan West於1394年出生在英國哈普爾時，她的父親Sir Thomas West 1st Baron West29歲，母親Joan la Warre31歲。她和Sir Richard de LA BERE, Lord of Kinnerseley至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1450年在英國倫敦享年56歲。

zh-hans.Kinnersley Castle, Hereford, Herefordshi: 英国英格兰賀福爾金納斯利
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan West于1394年出生在英国哈普爾时，她的父亲Sir Thomas West 1st Baron West29岁，母亲Joan la Warre31岁。她和Sir Richard de LA BERE, Lord of Kinnerseley至少有1个儿子和1个女儿。她去世于1450年在英国倫敦享年56岁。

nameMeanings.name: West
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and German: from Middle English, Middle High German west ‘west’, hence a topographic name for someone who lived to the west of a settlement, or a habitational name for someone who had migrated from further west.
Americanized form of Finnish Vesterinen: from the personal name Vesteri (a short form of Sylvester ) + the surname suffix -nen.

History: This name was brought to North America independently by many bearers in the 17th and 18th centuries. Thomas West, 12th Baron De La Warre, was captain general of Virginia in 1610–11. The state of DE is named for him. One of the earliest permanent settlers was Francis West (1606–92), who came to Duxbury, MA, from Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, in or before 1638.

variants.name: Western
meanings: English: from Middle English westerne ‘western’, denoting ‘(the man from the) west’. Compare West and Wester .
Norwegian: habitational name from the farm name Vestern, found in two places, a compound of vest ‘west’ + vin ‘meadow’, denoting a farm to the west.

variants.name: Sylvester
meanings: English and German: from the Middle English and Latin personal name Silvester ‘dweller in the forest’ (a derivative of silva ‘wood’). The name was borne by three popes, including a contemporary of Constantine the Great, and seems to have been first used in England by clerics.

variants.name: Westman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Birgit, Erik, Hilmer, Nels.
Swedish: ornamental or topographic name from (an altered form of) the elements väst ‘west’ + -man. It is also found in Finland.
Americanized form of German Westmann: variant of West , with the addition of Middle High German man ‘man’.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Joan West was born in 1394, in Hampshire, England, her father, Sir Thomas West 1st Baron West, was 29 and her mother, Joan la Warre, was 31. She had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Sir Richard de LA BERE, Lord of Kinnerseley. She died in 1450, in London, England, at the age of 56.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan West est née en 1394, à Hampshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Thomas West 1st Baron West, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Joan la Warre, avait 31 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Sir Richard de LA BERE, Lord of Kinnerseley. Elle est décédée en 1450, à Londres, Angleterre, à l’âge de 56 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan West 1394 in Hampshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Thomas West 1st Baron West 29 und ihre Mutter Joan la Warre 31. Sie hatte mit Sir Richard de LA BERE, Lord of Kinnerseley mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1450 im Alter von 56 Jahren in London, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan West è nata il nel 1394 nel Hampshire, England, sua padre, Sir Thomas West 1st Baron West, aveva 29 anni e sua madre, Joan la Warre, aveva 31 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia con Sir Richard de LA BERE, Lord of Kinnerseley. Morì nel 1450, nel London, England, all'età di 56 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Westが1394年にイギリスハンプシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas West 1st Baron Westは29歳，母Joan la Warreは31歳であった。彼女はSir Richard de LA BERE, Lord of Kinnerseleyとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1450年にイギリスロンドンで56歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan West(이)가 1394년 영국 함프실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Thomas West 1st Baron West(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan la Warre(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 Sir Richard de LA BERE, Lord of Kinnerseley(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1450년 영국 런던에서 56세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan West nasceu em 1394, em Hampshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Thomas West 1st Baron West, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Joan la Warre, tinha 31 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha com Sir Richard de LA BERE, Lord of Kinnerseley. Ela faleceu em 1450, em London, Inglaterra, com 56 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan West родилась в 1394 г. в местности хампшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Thomas West 1st Baron West было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan la Warre, 31. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь от Sir Richard de LA BERE, Lord of Kinnerseley. Она умерла в 1450 г. в местности Лондон, Англия в возрасте 56 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan West nació en 1394, en Hampshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Thomas West 1st Baron West, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Joan la Warre, tenía 31 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija con Sir Richard de LA BERE, Lord of Kinnerseley. Murió en 1450, en London, Inglaterra, a la edad de 56 años.
